data.content:
### Generating Mock Stripe Event for Connected Account

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/README.md

Use the Stripe CLI to trigger a mock `customer.created` event for a specific connected account. This simulates an action that would add the account to the backend's data storage and make it visible in the app frontend.

```Bash
stripe trigger --stripe-account CONNECTED_ACCOUNT_ID customer.created
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node.js Backend API dependencies

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/crm-full-stack/README.md

Navigate into the `nodejs-backend` directory and install all required Node.js packages using Yarn.

```Shell
cd nodejs-backend
yarn
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Postgres Database URL for Node.js Backend

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/crm-full-stack/README.md

Create a `.env` file in the `nodejs-backend` project root and add the connection string for your local Postgres database. Replace `postgres:postgres` with your actual username and password if different.

```dotenv
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/crmbuddy
```

--------------------------------

### Run Node.js Backend API development server

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/crm-full-stack/README.md

Start the Node.js backend API server in development mode. This command typically watches for file changes and restarts the server automatically.

```Shell
yarn run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Stripe App dependencies

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/crm-full-stack/README.md

Navigate into the `stripe-app` directory and install all required packages using Yarn.

```Shell
cd stripe-app
yarn
```

--------------------------------

### Start frontend or backend individually

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/github-oauth/README.md

These commands allow you to start either the frontend or the backend application independently. Use `yarn run start:frontend` for the frontend and `yarn run start:backend` for the backend.

```zsh
yarn run start:frontend
yarn run start:backend
```

--------------------------------

### Start frontend and backend applications concurrently

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/github-oauth/README.md

This command starts both the frontend and backend applications simultaneously using yarn's configured scripts. The backend server will be accessible on `localhost:8080`.

```zsh
yarn start
```

--------------------------------

### Run Stripe App development server

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/crm-full-stack/README.md

Start the Stripe App in development mode. This command typically launches the app and provides a local URL for access.

```Shell
yarn run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Dropbox App Client ID in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/dropbox-oauth-pkce/README.md

This snippet demonstrates how to set the Dropbox app's client ID in a `config.ts` file. This client ID, obtained from the Dropbox developer console, is essential for initiating the OAuth 2.0 PKCE flow.

```typescript
export const client_id='random_clientID'
```

--------------------------------

### Integrating ClimateLinkComponent in UI

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/climate/README.md

To integrate the Stripe Climate link into your own app, copy the component files and add a reference to the ClimateLinkComponent in your UI, providing your application ID.

```TypeScript
    <ClimateLinkComponent appId="<YOUR APP ID HERE>" />
```

--------------------------------

### Install and hoist project dependencies

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/github-oauth/README.md

This command installs and hoists all necessary dependencies for the project using yarn. It should be run from the root folder before starting the application.

```zsh
yarn
```

--------------------------------

### Setup and Run OAuth2 Stripe App Example

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/basic-auth/README.md

Detailed steps to set up and run the Stripe App and its Node.js backend for OAuth2 authentication. This includes uploading the app, generating local TLS certificates, configuring redirect URLs, setting necessary environment variables, and starting both the backend and frontend components.

```Shell
# 1. Upload the app to get APP_SECRET
stripe apps upload

# 2. Create local private key and certificate for TLS (e.g., using mkcert)
#    Save them in the backend folder as `key.pem` and `cert.pem`
#    Example using mkcert:
#    mkcert -install
#    mkcert localhost 127.0.0.1 ::1
#    # Move generated certs to backend folder and rename to key.pem/cert.pem

# 3. Add "https://localhost:8080/auth/callback/logged-in" as a valid redirect URL
#    in your auth server configuration.

# 4. Set the following environment variables:
export AUTH_HOST="example.us.auth0.com" # Replace with your auth server hostname
export CLIENT_ID="YOUR_CLIENT_ID"       # Get from your authentication server
export CLIENT_SECRET="YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET" # Get from your authentication server
export APP_SECRET="YOUR_APP_SECRET"     # Get from uploaded app's details view
export STRIPE_API_KEY="YOUR_STRIPE_API_KEY" # Get from Stripe developer dashboard

# 5. Run the backend server
yarn dev

# 6. Run the frontend
stripe apps start
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy API Service: Navigate and Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/send-mail/README.md

This snippet shows how to navigate into the API directory and install its required dependencies using Yarn. This is the initial step before configuring and running the Express application.

```bash
cd api
yarn
```

--------------------------------

### Set Non-Expiring Secret via cURL

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/secret-store/backend/README.md

This cURL command sends a POST request to the /set_secret endpoint to store a new secret. It includes the secret name, value, and an associated user ID. The secret will not have an expiration date.

```bash
curl localhost:4242/set_secret -d "secret_name"="name" -d "secret_value"="shh" -d "user_id"="usr_5"
```

--------------------------------

### Install Ruby Dependencies for Sinatra App

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/backend/ruby/README.md

Installs the necessary Ruby gems and dependencies for the Sinatra application using Bundler, ensuring all required libraries are available before running the server.

```bash
bundle install
```

--------------------------------

### Copy .env example file for backend setup

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/settings-view/bring-your-own-backend/README.md

This command copies the `.env.example` file to a `.env` file within the chosen backend server directory (e.g., `backend/node`). This is a crucial step for configuring environment variables such as `APP_SECRET` and `STRIPE_API_KEY` required by the backend.

```bash
cp .env.example backend/node/.env
```

--------------------------------

### Install PHP Dependencies with Composer

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/backend/php/README.md

Installs the required PHP dependencies for the application using Composer. This command should be run in the project's root directory.

```bash
composer install
```

--------------------------------

### List Secrets via cURL

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/secret-store/backend/README.md

This cURL command sends a GET request to the /list_secrets endpoint to retrieve all secrets associated with a specific user ID. The user ID is passed as a query parameter in the URL.

```bash
curl "localhost:4242/list_secrets?user_id=usr_5"
```

--------------------------------

### Set Dropbox App Redirect URI

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/dropbox-oauth-pkce/README.md

This is the specific redirect URI that must be configured in the Dropbox app settings. It ensures that after successful authentication, the OAuth flow redirects back to the Stripe Dashboard's test environment.

```plaintext
https://dashboard.stripe.com/test/apps-oauth/com.example.dropbox-oauth-pkce
```

--------------------------------

### Run Express API Service Locally

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/send-mail/README.md

This snippet provides the command to start the Express API application locally. This is useful for testing the API functionality and integration before deploying it to a hosting service.

```bash
node index.js
```

--------------------------------

### Set Expiring Secret via cURL

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/secret-store/backend/README.md

This cURL command sends a POST request to the /set_secret endpoint to store a new secret with a specified expiry timestamp. The secret will automatically be removed after the given Unix timestamp.

```bash
curl localhost:4242/set_secret -d "secret_name"="name" -d "secret_value"="shh" -d "user_id"="usr_5" -d "secret_expiry"=3490367953
```

--------------------------------

### Run PHP Application with Composer

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/backend/php/README.md

Starts the PHP application server, making it accessible on localhost:8080. Ensure all dependencies are installed before running this command.

```bash
composer start
```

--------------------------------

### Install project dependencies

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/settings-view/bring-your-own-backend/backend/node/README.md

Installs all required Node.js packages for the Express server using Yarn. This command fetches and sets up the necessary libraries defined in the project's package.json file.

```bash
yarn
```

--------------------------------

### Find Secret via cURL

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/secret-store/backend/README.md

This cURL command sends a GET request to the /find_secret endpoint to retrieve a secret. It requires the secret name and user ID as query parameters in the URL.

```bash
curl "localhost:4242/find_secret?secret_name=name&user_id=usr_5"
```

--------------------------------

### Starting Stripe App Development Server

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/linking-different-stripe-object/README.md

This command starts the Stripe Apps development server, allowing you to run and test your Stripe application locally. It requires the Stripe Apps CLI plugin to be installed and dependencies to be installed via 'yarn'.

```Shell
stripe apps start
```

--------------------------------

### Running Node.js Backend Server

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/README.md

Navigate to the specific backend language directory (e.g., Node.js) and install dependencies, then start the server. This example demonstrates the steps for a Node.js backend, but similar steps apply to other languages.

```Bash
cd backend/node
yarn
yarn start
```

--------------------------------

### Running the Stripe App in Preview Mode

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/ship-io/README.md

This command allows developers to start the Stripe App in preview mode, enabling them to see the application in action and test its UI functionality locally.

```Shell
stripe apps start
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Secret via cURL

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/secret-store/backend/README.md

This cURL command sends a POST request to the /delete_secret endpoint to remove a secret. It requires the secret name and user ID to be provided as form data.

```bash
curl localhost:4242/delete_secret -d "secret_name"="name" -d "user_id"="usr_5"
```

--------------------------------

### Installing Frontend Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/README.md

From the `frontend` folder, install all necessary project dependencies using Yarn. This prepares the frontend for local development and preview, ensuring all required packages are available.

```Bash
yarn
```

--------------------------------

### Start Stripe App for Preview

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/super-todo/README.md

Command to preview the Stripe app example. This command launches the app locally, allowing you to see the UI views in action without requiring specific application logic.

```Shell
stripe apps start
```

--------------------------------

### Copying Environment Configuration File

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/README.md

Before running the backend server, copy the example environment file to a new .env file within your chosen backend language's directory. This file will store sensitive API keys and webhook secrets required for the application.

```Bash
cp .env.example backend/java/app/.env
```

--------------------------------

### Setup and Run Flask Server Example

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/backend/python/README.md

This snippet provides commands for setting up a Python virtual environment, installing dependencies, and running the Flask server example. It includes specific instructions for MacOS/Unix (Bash) and Windows (PowerShell) environments, ensuring the server starts on port 8080.

```bash
python3 -m venv env
source env/bin/activate

pip install -r requirements.txt

export FLASK_APP=index.py
python3 -m flask run --port=8080
```

```powershell
python3 -m venv env
.\env\Scripts\activate.bat

pip install -r requirements.txt

$env:FLASK_APP="server.py"
python3 -m flask run --port=8080
```

--------------------------------

### Update TypeScript Hook to Call External Email API

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/send-mail/README.md

This snippet illustrates how to modify the `src/hooks/sendMail.ts` file to replace placeholder logic with an actual `fetch` call to the deployed custom email API. This enables the Stripe application to send real emails using the configured backend service.

```diff
-            setErrorMessage([
-                "API will called by these props",
-                JSON.stringify({
-                    from: fromAddress,
-                    to: customer.email,
-                    subject,
-                    text
-                }, null, 2),
-                "If you want to send a real email, please update the code to call the real API."
-            ].join('\n'))
-            setSendingStatus('error')
-            return;
-            /**
-             * @TODO replace the API to send a real email
-            fetch('https://example.com', {
+            fetch('https://YOUR-CUSTOM-API-URL', {
...
-           */
        },
        sendEmailErrorMessage: errorMessage,
        sendingStatus,
        subject,
        setSubject,
        text,
        setText,
    }
```

--------------------------------

### Start Express Server with Stripe API Key

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/secret-store/backend/README.md

This command starts the Node.js Express application, passing the Stripe API key as an environment variable. Ensure the STRIPE_TEST_KEY environment variable is properly set before execution.

```bash
STRIPE_API_KEY=$STRIPE_TEST_KEY node app.js
```

--------------------------------

### Run Tests for Stripe Apps UI Example

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/ui-testing/README.md

Execute this command from the `stripe-apps/examples/ui-testing` directory to run the UI tests for the example application, including snapshot tests.

```Shell
yarn test
```

--------------------------------

### Run Ruby Sinatra Webhook Server

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/backend/ruby/README.md

Starts the Ruby Sinatra web server application, making it accessible on localhost:8080. This command initiates the server process that handles webhook interactions.

```bash
ruby server.rb
```

--------------------------------

### Install Gradle Dependencies for Java Server

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/backend/java/README.md

This command installs the necessary dependencies for the Java server application using Gradle. It should be run from the Java directory of the project.

```bash
gradle install
```

--------------------------------

### Run Java Server Application with Gradle

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/backend/java/README.md

This command starts the Java server application using the Gradle wrapper. Once executed, the server will be running and accessible on `localhost:8080`.

```bash
./gradlew run
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Run Node.js Webhook Server

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/install-webhooks/backend/node/README.md

Commands to install project dependencies and start the Node.js Express server for the webhook example.

```Bash
npm install
```

```Bash
npm start
```

--------------------------------

### Running Stripe Apps CLI Command

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/climate/README.md

After installing dependencies, use this command to start the Stripe App via the Stripe Apps CLI plugin.

```Shell
stripe apps start
```

--------------------------------

### Update Stripe App Content Security Policy for External API

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/send-mail/README.md

This snippet shows the necessary modification to the `stripe-app.json` file to register the custom API URL in the Content Security Policy. This allows the Stripe application to make requests to the deployed external API.

```diff
    "content_security_policy": {
-      "connect-src": [],
+      "connect-src": ["https://YOUR-CUSTOM-API-URL"],
      "purpose": ""
    }
```

--------------------------------

### Start Stripe Apps UI Testing Example

Source: https://github.com/stripe/stripe-apps/blob/main/examples/ui-testing/README.md

This command initiates the Stripe Apps UI testing example locally. It should be run from the `stripe-apps/examples/ui-testing` directory after installing project dependencies.

```Shell
stripe apps start
```
